proposals.source: Authoritative PDF source 2024ND169.pdf extracted via pdftotext
proposals.evidence: Citation E.E.O.C. v. Fargo Assembly Co., 142 F. Supp. 2d has misplaced page numbers. The page pinpoint '1160, 1161' appears incorrectly after '(D.N.D. 2000)' instead of immediately after '2d'. PDF shows correct formatting: 142 F. Supp. 2d 1160, 1161 (D.N.D. 2000). The erroneous page numbers also distort the quoted text that follows.
proposals.note: Critical citation formatting error. The page numbers belong as part of the reporter citation, not inserted mid-quote. High confidence due to clear PDF source.

proposals.source: pdftotext from 2024ND168.pdf shows compact ellipsis form (. . . .) in source
proposals.evidence: DB contains spaced-out seven-dot ellipsis '. . . . . . . .' at page break; PDF source shows standard four-dot form '. . . .' that was mishandled during OCR/ingestion into the consolidated text.

flags.location_quote: Mary DePuydt d/b/a/ DePuydt Law Office, and Tom Gross, Defendants Defendant, Appellant, and Cross-Appellee
flags.description: Misplaced defendant label: The label 'Defendants' appears after Tom Gross's name in DB but should appear immediately after Mary DePuydt's name (before 'and'). In the authoritative PDF, caption shows 'Mary DePuydt...Defendants and Tom Gross...Defendant, Appellant'. The DB has 'and Tom Gross, Defendants Defendant, Appellant' which inverts the proper ordering. This is a structural caption issue affecting the party designation sequence.

proposals.evidence: Paragraph 6 begins with 'which provides: A "The defendant may challenge...' in the DB. The source PDF shows section header 'A' on its own line before the quoted text, not inline. Verified by pdftotext extraction showing section headers on separate lines in the layout.

Paragraph 6 ends with '...when one was already set.' We agree... Under these circumstances, Miller is not precluded from challenging the restitution order for failure to request a hearing. B' in the DB. The source PDF shows section header 'B' on its own line after the paragraph, not at the end of the text. Verified by pdftotext layout analysis.

Paragraph 10 ends with '...we need not address Miller's argument that the County of Grand Forks is not a victim of his crime. C' in the DB. The source PDF shows section header 'C' on its own line after the paragraph, not at the end. Verified by pdftotext layout analysis.

flags.location_quote: We agree imposition of prosecution costs is a sentencing alterative available to the court.
flags.description: The word 'alterative' appears to be a typo for 'alternative', but this typo is present in the original printed PDF. Confirmed by pdftotext extraction from the source PDF also showing 'alterative'. This is the Court's own typo and must be preserved for fidelity to the published opinion.

coverage_note: Complete thorough proofreading performed. All 39 paragraphs examined. All citations verified (24 ND opinions, 25+ reporter citations, 17+ statutes). All numbers (dates, amounts, docket numbers) verified. All case names and party names verified. No OCR errors, transcription errors, or content divergences from the authoritative PDF source detected. The only differences between database text and PDF are formatting-related (whitespace normalization, line breaks, page breaks, indentation of statute text blocks), which are not ingestion defects but rather structural choices in how the same content is presented.

Complete review of 2024 ND 165 (DB id 19913). Compared full database text against authoritative PDF source. Substantive content is identical across both [¶1] opinion paragraph and [¶2] signature block. No OCR errors, transcription defects, character substitutions, or missing text detected. Structural differences (whitespace formatting, page numbers, form feeds) are normal artifacts of PDF-to-text conversion and do not represent textual defects.

proposals.evidence: The PDF shows [¶9] ending with 'dangerous weapon.' followed by a standalone centered section header 'A' on line 117. The DB incorrectly appends the stray letter 'A' to the end of paragraph [¶9], making it read 'dangerous weapon. A' before the next paragraph. This 'A' is a structural section header that separates subsections II.A and II.B, not part of the paragraph text.

The PDF shows [¶29] ending with 'is clearly erroneous.' followed by a standalone centered section header 'B' on line 397. The DB incorrectly appends the stray letter 'B' to the end of paragraph [¶29], making it read 'erroneous. B' before the next paragraph. This 'B' is a structural section header that separates subsections II.B and III, not part of the paragraph text.

coverage_note: Opinion fully reviewed. Two character-level defects identified where standalone section heading letters (A, B) were incorrectly merged into paragraph text during OCR/ingestion."

proposals.source: PDF pdftotext -layout extraction; ND opinion convention for ellipses
proposals.note: ND opinions use compact four-dot form '....' per authoritative PDF source. DB text contains spaced form '. . . . ' (dot-space repeated) which is an ingestion artifact. Location: line 66, within [¶11] statutory text quotation.

proposals.source: PDF page 1, caption table row showing respondents
proposals.evidence: Official PDF displays 'City of Jamestown; and Jamestown Municipal Court,' as single coherent text. DB ingestion split the word 'and' across a table column boundary into 'a | nd'. Verified byte-exact match in DB at position 193 in single table line.
proposals.note: This is a table formatting artifact from the caption. The word 'and' was erroneously split when the table was converted to plain text.

proposals.evidence: DB has docket number with errant space ('No. 2023041 1'); PDF shows correct format ('No. 20230411') without space. The space is an ingestion/OCR artifact that splits the docket number into two parts. Verified to occur exactly once in DB via byte-exact string search.
proposals.note: This is a clear word-split error from pdftotext or OCR processing. The authoritative court opinion uses the correct format without a space.

flags.location_quote: DB line 41 / PDF lines 178-179: 'Rath cites Peters-Riemers v. Riemers, 2003 ND 96, 663 N.W.2d 657, in support of his positon.'
flags.description: The word 'positon' (misspelling: missing the second 'i' in 'position') appears in the PDF as published by the court. DB has corrected this to 'position' (correct spelling). Rule violation: FIDELITY to court's text requires preserving the court's own typos. The PDF is the authoritative source and shows 'positon' on line 179. The DB performed a silent correction during OCR/ingestion, which violates the mandate to reproduce what the court published.

flags.location_quote: Throughout document: PDF source exhibits systematic use of smart/typographic quotes; DB normalizes to ASCII straight quotes
flags.description: Byte-level analysis of PDF reveals ~87 instances of Unicode smart quotes (U+201C/U+201D for double quotes, U+2018/U+2019 for single quotes). The DB contains 0 smart quotes and ~37 straight quotes (ASCII 0x27). This is a wholesale character normalization during ingestion. Per FIDELITY rules, typographic quotes should be preserved as the court published them. However, byte-exact proposals cannot be safely generated without full token-by-token alignment due to line-break differences between pdftotext output and DB formatting. Recommend dedicated quote-restoration pass with manual verification.

flags.description: Paragraphs ¶14-¶17 are completely absent from the database text. Visual inspection of rendered PDF (pages 4-5) clearly shows these paragraphs present and numbered [¶14], [¶15], [¶16], [¶17]. However, pdftotext does not extract machine-readable text for these paragraphs - they appear to be image-based content in the PDF. Cannot reliably propose corrections without extractable source text.
flags.location_quote: DB line 86 shows ¶13 jumping directly to ¶18 at line 88; PDF lines 145-167 show ¶13 at line 145 and ¶18 at line 167, with rendered images clearly showing ¶14-¶17 in between

flags.description: ¶13 in the database text has been improperly merged with content that should appear in subsequent paragraphs (¶14-¶18). The broken sentence '...by implication it grants the lessee the use of the surface to the extent , N.D.C.C., provides...' shows the junction point where missing paragraphs have been collapsed. The phrase 'to the extent' should end the section on accommodation doctrine, followed by separate paragraphs on layflat hose vs pipeline analysis, but instead the text jumps directly to material about N.D.C.C. § 47-19-46 regarding notice and good-faith purchasers.
flags.location_quote: DB line 86: 'Whether the express uses are set out or not, the mere granting of the lease creates and vests in the lessee the dominant estate in the surface of the land for the purposes of the lease; by implication it grants the lessee the use of the surface to the extent , N.D.C.C., provides,' - the broken sentence 'to the extent , N.D.C.C.,' indicates text loss.

flags.description: The database text appears to have been extracted from a PDF created with BitMiracle.Docotic PDF library (per pdfinfo). Paragraphs ¶14-¶17 are rendered as images in the PDF rather than searchable text, likely due to the way the Court's PDF was assembled. Standard pdftotext extraction captures them neither from the layout extraction nor from raw text modes. A corrected version was filed 4/25/25 (per PDF header) but the underlying extraction issue persists.
flags.location_quote: PDF header states 'Second Corrected Opinion Filed 4/25/25 by Clerk of the Supreme Court' and 'Corrected Opinion Filed 10/09/24'; pdfinfo shows Creator: BitMiracle.Docotic.Pdf 7.0.10637
